title: P2 Infrastructure Fixes — AudioManager error state reset, HomeScreen daily word count refresh, Playwright mobile viewport
approach_rationale: Approach B (Ideal) is chosen over A (Conservative) because Iron Law 4 requires every feature/behavioral change to have at least one test. The Howler mock and Jest infrastructure are already in place, making test authoring low-effort. The three behavioral changes (error reset, visibility listener, viewport fix) each warrant targeted tests.

files_to_modify.change: Reset this.error flag and evict stale cache entries at the start of playWordEn and playWord to allow successful retries after a previous load failure.

Extract the data-fetch IIFE into a named fetchData function; add a visibilitychange event listener that calls fetchData when document.visibilityState becomes 'visible'; remove the listener in the cleanup return.
Remove '...devices["Desktop Chrome"]' spread from the projects[0].use object so the globally configured 375×667 viewport is not overridden.

files_to_create.purpose: Unit tests verifying AudioManager error-reset behaviour: (1) playWordEn resets error flag and evicts stale cache before retrying, (2) playWord resets error flag and evicts stale cache before retrying.
Unit tests verifying (1) visibilitychange triggers data re-fetch when state becomes 'visible', (2) the listener is cleaned up on unmount (removeEventListener called).
Playwright E2E test that asserts page.viewportSize() returns { width: 375, height: 667 }, confirming the Desktop Chrome override is gone.

steps.action: Modify src/lib/audio.ts — add error reset + cache eviction to playWordEn and playWord
Modify src/components/HomeScreen.tsx — extract fetchData, add visibilitychange listener + cleanup
Write src/__tests__/audio.test.ts with unit tests for error-reset behavior
Write src/__tests__/HomeScreen.test.tsx with unit tests for visibility change
Write tests/e2e/viewport.spec.ts with viewport assertion test
Run npm test (Jest) to verify all unit tests pass

cases: playWordEn: resets error flag before new attempt
playWordEn: evicts stale cache entry when error was set
playWordEn: does not evict cache entry when no prior error
playWord: resets error flag before new attempt
playWord: evicts stale cache entries for both paths when error was set
re-fetches data when visibilityState changes to visible
does not re-fetch when visibilityState changes to hidden

risk_areas.risk: The audioManager export is a module-level singleton; Jest module isolation may share state between tests. Mitigation: reset the singleton's internal state via the public API between tests, or use jest.resetModules() / jest.isolateModules().
HomeScreen depends on useDB (returns IndexedDB instance) and db module functions (getSetting, getAllSessions). These must be mocked carefully to avoid real IndexedDB calls.
risk_areas.mitigation: Mock useDB to return a truthy db object; mock getSetting and getAllSessions with jest.fn() returning resolved promises.

risk_areas.risk: The listener fires asynchronously; tests must use waitFor or act() to flush the async fetchData call.
risk_areas.mitigation: Wrap fireEvent dispatch + assertion in act() + waitFor.

risk_areas.risk: HomeScreen.tsx grows from 145 to ~155 lines. The sanity.spec.ts test enforces ≤200 lines for files in src/components/. 155 < 200, so this passes.
risk_areas.mitigation: None needed; 155 is comfortably under the 200-line limit.

risk_areas.risk: audio.ts grows from 107 to ~121 lines. Requirement says it must remain under 200 lines.
The viewport test relies on the web server being up. It will naturally run with the fixed config, so the test itself validates the fix.
risk_areas.mitigation: Use the existing webServer config which re-uses an existing dev server.

persistence_strategy: N/A — these changes are runtime behavior fixes. Data persistence is handled by the existing IndexedDB layer (getAllSessions, getSetting). The visibilitychange listener reads fresh data from IndexedDB each time.
error_handling_approach: playWordEn already has a try/catch; playWord has loaderror callbacks. The HomeScreen fetchData is already wrapped in try/catch with console.warn. No new error paths are introduced.
assumptions: The task says 'prefer the empty-use approach' for playwright.config.ts. We use '{}' (not Pixel 5 device).
The visibilitychange refresh only needs to update todayWords and childName — no other HomeScreen data is missing.
The AudioManager error flag must be reset before every new play attempt (not just once), so both playWordEn and playWord get the reset.
The error reset in playWord should evict both enPath and zhPath cache entries (as described in the task).
Unit tests for HomeScreen only need to cover the visibility change behavior; full rendering tests for all other aspects are out of scope.
The E2E viewport test can live in a new file (tests/e2e/viewport.spec.ts) rather than expanding sanity.spec.ts.
